_meta.subtaskTitle: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners
_meta.parentTaskTitle: Study the meaning and conditions of La ilaha illAllah
_meta.claudeScholarQuery: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners' under the parent task 'Study the meaning and conditions of La ilaha illAllah' (module: faith_shahada_core), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus: (1) Quran-biased — name likely primary ayat that directly or contextually anchor this subtask; (2) Hadith-biased — name likely Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) that directly or contextually anchor this subtask. Return the queries inside a <queries></queries> block as a numbered list, each one sentence, ready to paste into a retrieval system. Do NOT answer the queries — query formulation only.

muslimScholarQueries.query: Retrieve primary Quranic verses (Arabic and English) that directly or contextually establish: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners
Retrieve Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) with grade that directly or contextually establish: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners

sources.arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
sources.translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
sources.rationale: The verse names the exclusive purpose of creation as worship of Allah — the scriptural foundation for the subtask's framing that worshipping Allah alone is not one option among many but the very reason for existence. Bayyinah (primary Quran); direct (operative statement of worship-only-Him).

notebookTrace.query: Retrieve primary Quranic verses (Arabic and English) that directly or contextually establish: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners

sources.arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ. اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ. لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ. وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
sources.translation: Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent.'
sources.rationale: Surah al-Ikhlas is the Quran's definitive statement of Allah's absolute oneness and incomparability — the negation of equivalents is precisely what 'without partners' means. Directly supplies the doctrinal content the subtask asks the user to understand. Bayyinah + direct.

notebookTrace.query: Retrieve primary Quranic verses (Arabic and English) that directly or contextually establish: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners

sources.ref: Sahih Muslim, Book 1, Number 0046 (narration of Mu'adh b. Jabal)
sources.translation: Allah's right on His slaves is that they should worship Him (Alone) and should not worship any besides Him.
sources.rationale: The Mu'adh narration states verbatim the operative ruling the subtask names — that the right Allah holds over His servants is to be worshipped alone, associating nothing with Him. Bayyinah (Sahih Muslim, Book 001 Number 0046 confirmed by MS reference chunk) + direct.

notebookTrace.query: Retrieve Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) with grade that directly or contextually establish: Understand what it means to worship Allah alone without partners

_groundedBarCheck.reason: 3 entries; all Bayyinah; all direct. Bar met. Rejected: Quran 21:25, 2:163, 39:2, 16:36, 4:36, 31:13 — while named inline in MS answer with Arabic and translation, MS reference chunks for these indices were null or pointed to general Shahada-introduction matter rather than the ayah chunks themselves. Kept 51:56 and 112:1-4 as the most canonical and thematically tightest anchors (no contrary disclaimer was issued in this q1 output, unlike 1-0 q1). Rejected: Bukhari Vol 4 Book 52 #107 (Sahl narration) — no verbatim matn surfaced in MS answer, only cited_text 'Volume 4, Book 52, Number 107: Narrated Sahl:' which cannot stand as matn.
